claim_raw: Many plan subcommand handlers call the bare load_plan() function, ignoring the --state-derived plan path. Only skip, unskip, and reopen in override_handlers.py correctly derive the plan file from the state path via _plan_file_for_state(). All other handlers — describe, note, reorder, queue, commit-log show/reset, and all cluster subcommands — load from the default PLAN_FILE regardless of the --state flag, creating a split-brain between plan reads/writes when a non-default state path is used.
claim_distilled: 19+ plan subcommand handlers use bare load_plan() ignoring the --state flag, while skip/unskip/reopen correctly derive the plan path from state — causing silent split-brain plan mutations when --state points to a non-default location.
claim_restated: When --state is passed to a non-default path (e.g. --state /project/.desloppify/state-python.json), plan_path_for_state() should derive the co-located plan.json. skip/unskip/reopen do this correctly via _plan_file_for_state(state_path). All other plan handlers (describe, note, reorder, queue, commit-log, show, reset, all 9+ cluster sub-handlers) call bare load_plan() which unconditionally loads the default PLAN_FILE, diverging from the state-path-derived plan. This is verified by direct code inspection of all six handler files.

evidence: reorder_handlers.py: cmd_plan_reorder called bare load_plan() (pre-fix line ~49)
queue_render.py: cmd_plan_queue called bare load_plan() (pre-fix line ~179)
commit_log_handlers.py: cmd_commit_log_dispatch called bare load_plan() (pre-fix line ~193)
cmd.py: _cmd_plan_show and _cmd_plan_reset both called bare load_plan() (pre-fix lines ~102, ~165)
cluster_handlers.py: 9 handlers all called bare load_plan() (_create, _add, _remove, _delete, _reorder, _show, _list, _update, _merge)
override_handlers.py: cmd_plan_describe and cmd_plan_note called bare load_plan() (pre-fix)
override_handlers.py: cmd_plan_skip/unskip/reopen correctly used _plan_file_for_state() — confirming the asymmetry
claude.notes: Claim is accurate and well-scoped. The asymmetry is real and systematic — 19+ call sites wrong vs 3 correct. Practical impact is limited to non-default --state configurations (per-language state files), but the pattern is architecturally wrong. Code refs in the submission match actual codebase structure well. Accuracy docked 1 point because some specific line numbers in the original submission were slightly off from actual pre-fix positions.
